corpus_building.implemented_as: The TXT paper is converted into a section-aware annotated mini-corpus.

tokenization.implemented_as: Every concept phrase and sentence is normalized into tokens for counts and n-grams.

normalization.implemented_as: Lower-casing and punctuation cleanup are used for phrase matching and deduplication.

n_gram.implemented_as: Concept length and document top unigrams/bigrams/trigrams are exposed.

bow_and_naive_bayes.implemented_as: Section BoW hits act as class-conditional section evidence for intro/method/experiment/conclusion style decisions.

tf_idf_document_term_matrix.implemented_as: Document-level frequency and TF-IDF matrices expose which section concepts are characteristic of this paper.

laplace_smoothing.implemented_as: P(section | concept) uses add-one smoothing, so unseen section-concept pairs are not forced to zero.

evidence_cue_lexicon.implemented_as: Cue phrases such as propose, achieve, outperform, and BLEU give transparent rule-based evidence hints.

sequence_labeling.implemented_as: The first model contributes BIO boundary evidence for concept phrase extraction.

canonicalization_noisy_channel_style.implemented_as: Observed surface phrases are mapped to canonical terms with an alias confidence score.

location.sentence: Learning effective latent dynamics models is becoming feasible through advances in deep learning and latent variable models (Krishnan et al., 2015; Karl et al., 2016; Doerr et al., 2018; Buesing et al., 2018).

location.sentence: For this, we propose an actor critic method that optimizes a parametric policy by propagating analytic gradients of multi-step values back through learned latent dynamics.

context_window.previous: 7 C ONCLUSION We present Dreamer, an agent that learns long-horizon behaviors purely by latent imagination.
context_window.current: For this, we propose an actor critic method that optimizes a parametric policy by propagating analytic gradients of multi-step values back through learned latent dynamics.
context_window.next: Dreamer outperforms previous methods in data-efficiency, computation time, and final performance on a variety of challenging continuous control tasks with image inputs.

location.sentence: Representation learning Dreamer can be used with any differentiable dynamics model that pre- dicts future rewards given actions and past observations.

top_candidates.sentence: Using the same hyper parameters for all tasks, Dreamer exceeds previous model-based and model-free agents in terms of data-efficiency, computation time, and final performance.

As outlined in Figure and detailed in Algorithm 1, Dreamer performs the following operations throughout the agent's life time, either interleaved or in parallel: • Learning the latent dynamics model from the dataset of past experience to predict future rewards from actions and past observations.
We present Dreamer, a reinforcement learning agent that solves long-horizon tasks from images purely by latent imagination.
On 20 challenging visual control tasks, Dreamer exceeds existing approaches in data-efficiency, computation time, and final performance.
POPLIN improves over online planning by self-imitation. learn robot policies by imagination with a latent dynamics model.

As outlined in Figure and detailed in Algorithm 1, Dreamer performs the following operations throughout the agent's life time, either interleaved or in parallel: • Learning the latent dynamics model from the dataset of past experience to predict future rewards from actions and past observations.

The value model optimizes Bellman consistency for imagined rewards and the action model is updated by propagating gradients of value estimates back through the neural network dynamics. • Executing the learned action model in the world to collect new experience for growing the dataset.

These trajectories branch off of the model states s t of sequence batches drawn from the agent's dataset of experience and predict forward for the imagination horizon H using actions sampled from the action model.
We present Dreamer, an agent that learns long-horizon behaviors from images purely by latent imagination.
A novel actor critic algorithm accounts for rewards beyond the imagination horizon while making efficient use of the neural network dynamics.
We present Dreamer, an agent that learns long-horizon behaviors purely by latent imagination.
For this, we propose an actor critic method that optimizes a parametric policy by propagating analytic gradients of multi-step values back through learned latent dynamics.
Dreamer outperforms previous methods in data-efficiency, computation time, and final performance on a variety of challenging continuous control tasks with image inputs.
We approach this limitation by predicting both actions and state values.
